notes: Rabindranath Tagore. He was also referred to as ‘Gurudev’, ‘Kabiguru’, and ‘Biswakabi’. He got Nobel Prize for his work Gitanjali. He was a Bengali poet, novelist, and painter, who was highly influential in introducing Indian culture to the west. He was the first non-European to receive the Nobel Prize.

Jai Prakash Narayan. He received the Ramon Magsaysay Award (1965) and Bharat Ratna (1998, posthumously). Famous Personalities & their Nicknames: Bihar Kesri - Sri Krishna Sinha, Bihar Vibhuti - Dr. Anurag Narayan Singh, Iron Man of India - Vallabhai Patel, Biswa Kavi - Rabindranath Tagore, Netaji - Subhash Chandra Bose. Lokmanya Tilak - Bal Gangadhar Tilak.

Iron man of India. Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel is popularly known as the "Iron Man of India" due to his crucial role in the unification and integration of Indian princely states such as Travancore, Hyderabad, Junagadh, Bhopal into the Indian Union. Other sobriquet personalities: Father of Indian Unrest - Bal Gangadhar Tilak, Grand old man of India - Dadabhai Naoroji, Morning star of Indian Renaissance - Raja Ram Mohan Roy.

Amar Jiban by Rassundari Devi. She was the first Indian woman to write an autobiography and the first Bengali to write an autobiography. Authors and Books: Baby Kamble - ‘’The Prisons We Broke’’. Lakshmibai Tilak - ‘’Smritichitre’’. Kamala Suraiyya - ‘’The Old Playhouse And Other Poems’’, ‘’Padmavati the Harlot and Other Stories’’. Q.1385. Who became the first Vice-Chancellor of Jawaharlal Nehru University in 1969 ? SSC CHSL 07/08/2023 (4th shift) (a) BD Nagchaudhuri (b) G Parthasarathi (c) Y Nayudamma (d) KR Narayanan Sol.1385.(b) G Parthasarathi. Jawaharlal Nehru University (JNU): A public major research university located in New Delhi, India. It was established in 1969 and named after Jawaharlal Nehru, India's first Prime Minister. Other Central Universities : Aligarh Muslim University (1875), Assam University, Banaras Hindu University (1916), Central University of Gujarat (2009). Q.1386. Who was the first woman graduate of Calcutta University ? SSC CHSL 08/08/2023 (1st shift) (a) Sarojini Naidu (b) Annie Besant (c) Kadambini Ganguly (d) Rajkumari Amritkaur Sol.1386.(c) Kadambini Ganguly and Chandramukhi Basu were the first BA graduates in the British empire in India. She was the South Asia's first female surgeon. Rajkumari Amritkaur - The first Health Minister of India (1947). Q.1387. Who among the following advocated the ideology of "oru jati, oru matam, oru daivam manushyanu" (one caste, one religion, one god for humankind)? SSC CGL 19/07/2023 (1st shift) (a) Birsa Munda (b) Narayana Guru (c) Swami Shraddhanand (d) Keshub Chunder Sen Sol.1387.(b) Narayana Guru. He belongs to Ezhava caste and was considered ‘avarna’. He built a temple dedicated to Lord Shiva at Aruvippuram which was against the caste based restrictions of the time. He established the Sree Narayana Dharma Paripalana Yogam (SNDP), a charitable society. His Books: “Advaitha Deepika”, “Asrama”and “Thevarappathinkangal”. He became the proponent of Advaita Vedanta (Principle of non-duality) put forward by Adi Shankara.

Kailash Satyarthi was awarded the Nobel Peace Prize in 2014 "for the struggle against the suppression of children and young people and the right of all children to education". He founded the Bachpan Bachao Andolan (Save the Childhood Movement) in 1980.

Pt Jasraj. The International Astronomical Union (IAU) has named minor planet VP32 (number - 300128), discovered on November 11, 2006, as 'Pandit Jasraj'. He became the first Indian musician to get honored with this unique recognition. Q.1390. Which of the following architects designed the Gateway of India? Higher Secondary 27/06/2023 (Shift - 3) (a) Herbert Baker (b) George Wittet (c) Henry Irwin (d) Lauri Baker Sol.1390.(b) George Wittet. Gateway of India (1924). The foundation stone was placed in in Bombay. The monument was erected to commemorate the landing of King George V and Queen Mary at Apollo Bunder in India. Other Architects and their works: Henry Irwin - Ambas Vilas Palace (Mysore). Lauri Baker - The Indian Coffee House, Central for Development Studies (Kerala). He was known as the “Brick Master of Kerala” and “Gandhi of Architecture”.
Kerala. Padma Bhushan (2024). First female judge of India - Justice Anna Chandy.

Italy. First Female Prime Minister of Some Countries: Austria - Brigitte Bierlein. Spain - María Teresa Fernández de la Vega. Ukraine - Tymoshenko. India - Indira Gandhi. Bangladesh - Sheikh Hasina Wazed. Pakistan - Benazier Bhutto. Q.1393. Fatima Sheikh was widely regarded as _______. SSC MTS 12/05/2023 (Morning) (a) India's First Female Muslim Entrepreneur (b) India's First Female Doctor (c) India's First Female Muslim Teacher (d) India's First Female Engineer Sol.1393.(c) India's First Female Muslim Teacher. She was honoured with a doodle on her 191st birth anniversary by Google On 9 January 2022. India's First Female Engineer - Ayyalasomayajula Lalitha. First Indian female doctor of western medicine - Anandi Gopal Joshi.
1861. Shiva Dayal Saheb founded the sect ‘Radha Soami Satsang’ on Basant Panchami. The sole purpose of this satsang is to experience the divinity of God who resides in all of us.
Filmmaker. Other Awards of Satyajit Ray: Padam Shri (1958), Padam Bhushan (1965), Dada Saheb Phalke award (1985), Ramon Magsaysay Award (1967) and Bharat Ratna (1992).

Shri Krishna Sinha was also known as Bihar Kesari and Shri Babu. He served as the first Chief Minister of Bihar from 1946 to 1961. Nicknames of other personalities - Rajendra Prasad (Desh Ratna, Ajatshatru), Karpuri Thakur (Jana Nayak), Spacing -Lala Lajpat Rai (Punjab Kesari).

Homi J. Bhabha. Bhabha Atomic Research Centre (Mumbai - 1954), Tata Institute of Fundamental Research (Mumbai - 1945), ISRO (Bangalore - 1969), DRDO (New Delhi - 1958). Vikram Sarabhai - Father of Indian Space Programme. APJ Abdul Kalam - Missile Man of India.

A. P. J. Abdul Kalam. IGMDP was an Indian Ministry of Defence programme to research and develop a comprehensive range of missiles. The project started in 1982 - (completed in 2008) under the leadership of Dr APJ Abdul Kalam (missile man of India). Prithvi, Agni, Trishul, Akash, Nag missiles have been developed under this.

Indira Gandhi. She was the third prime minister of India from 1966 to and from until her assassination in 1984. She was India's first and, to date, only female prime minister of India. Awards - Bharat Ratna (1971), and Bangladesh Freedom Honour (2011). Mary Kom - The first Indian woman boxer to win an olympic medal and a gold at Asian Games.

Amir Khusro: He was a poet in Delhi Sultanate. He is sometimes referred to as the "voice of India" or "Parrot of India" (Tuti-e-Hind), and has been called the "Father of Urdu literature”. He was a mystic and a spiritual disciple of Nizamuddin Auliya of Delhi.

Sunil Gavasker. Haryana Hurricane - Kapil Dev, Captain Cool - Mahendra Singh Dhoni, The Wall - Rahul Dravid, Jumbo - Anil Kumble, Turbanator - Harbhajan Singh, Master Blaster - Sachin Tendulkar, Hit - Man - Rohit Sharma, Nawab of Nazafgarh - Virendra Sehwag.
Sukhbir Singh. He was nominated and won the 1996 Channel V Awards in three categories: Best Debut Album, Best Male Vocalist and Best Music Video (for "Punjabi Munde") for his first album ‘New Style’.
Le Corbusier. Chandigarh city was designed by Le Corbusier . Matthew Nowicki was chief architect of the new Indian city of Chandigarh. Albert Mayer known for plan of Chandigarh.
Morarji Desai. He was leading the government formed by the Janata Party. He is the oldest person to hold the office of prime minister, at the age of 81, in the history of Indian politics.
Premchand was an Indian writer known for his modern Hindustani literature.
Nagmani. Amrita Pritam was an Indian novelist, essayist and poet, who wrote in Punjabi and Hindi. She edited Nagmani, a monthly literary magazine in Punjabi for several years.
Cartoonist. Mario de Miranda : Awards - Padma Shri (1988), Padma Bhushan (2002), Padma Vibhushan (2012).
2 October 1904. He was born in Mughalsarai, UP. He served as the second Prime Minister of India. The Iconic slogan “Jai Jawan Jai Kisaan” was given by him.
Maharashtra. She was the first female teacher of India.
